attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a heated apple on the table, left of the sponge.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene is set in a kitchen environment with a dining table and a kitchen counter area featuring a stove and microwave. The task is to put a heated apple on the table, to the left of the sponge.

**Objects and Types:**

- **Receptacles:**
  - `microwave`: A silver microwave oven mounted above the stove. It is a receptacle and is openable.
  - `sink`: Not visible in the provided images, but implied as a receptacle type in the domain.
  - `fridge`: Not visible in the provided images, but implied as a receptacle type in the domain.
  - `table`: The green table surface is not formally defined as a receptacle in the domain, but objects are placed on it. For planning purposes, it can be treated as a surface to place objects, though not a receptacle per the domain definition.

- **Objects:**
  - `apple`: A red apple, located on the table in the first image. It is not sliced, not heated, and not cooled. It is an object that can be held and heated.
  - `knife`: A knife with a yellow handle is visible on the table in the second image, near the loaf of bread. It is an object and can be used for slicing.
  - `sponge`: A green rectangular sponge with a yellow border is on the table. It is an object. The instruction requires placing the heated apple to its left.
  - `wine bottle`: A dark green bottle on the table, not relevant to the task.
  - `lettuce`: A head of green lettuce on the table, not relevant.
  - `fork`: A fork is on the table, near the apple in the first image.
  - `spatula`: A spatula with a black handle and red shaft is on the table.
  - `grill pan`: A brown grill pan is on the right side of the table.
  - `loaf of bread`: A brown loaf of bread is on the table in the second image.
  - `spoon`: A spoon is on the table in the second image.
  - `mug`: Two mugs are visible on the shelf under the table in the second image.
  - `utensil holder`: A brown container holding spoons and spatulas is on the counter next to the stove.

**Quantities:**

- 1 apple
- 1 knife (with yellow handle)
- 1 sponge (green with yellow border)
- 1 microwave
- 1 sink (implied, not visible)
- 1 fridge (implied, not visible)
- 1 table (surface)
- 1 wine bottle
- 1 head of lettuce
- 1 fork
- 1 spatula
- 1 grill pan
- 1 loaf of bread
- 1 spoon
- 2 mugs
- 1 utensil holder

**Spatial Relationships:**

- In the first image, the apple is on the table, to the right of the sponge. The sponge is positioned near the center of the table, and the apple is to its right.
- In the second image, the apple remains on the table, still to the right of the sponge. The knife is now visible on the table, to the left of the sponge and near the loaf of bread.
- The microwave is above the stove, which is on the counter. The stove has two burners and control knobs.
- The table has two wooden chairs behind it.
- The sponge is on the table, and the apple needs to be placed to its left after being heated.

**Current State of Objects Relevant to the Task:**

- The apple is on the table, not held by any agent, not heated, not sliced, not cooled.
- The sponge is on the table, not held, not heated, not sliced.
- The microwave is not opened, not toggled, and not currently in use.
- The knife is on the table, not held, not used for slicing yet.
- The agent is not at any location initially.

**Visual Properties:**

- The apple is bright red and round.
- The sponge is green with a yellow border, rectangular.
- The knife has a yellow handle and a metallic blade.
- The microwave is silver with a black control panel.
- The table is green with a checkered pattern on part of its surface.
- The chairs are wooden with curved backrests.
- The grill pan is brown with ridges.
- The loaf of bread is brown and oval-shaped.

The task requires the robot to pick up the apple, heat it in the microwave, and then place it on the table to the left of the sponge. The current position of the apple is to the right of the sponge, so it must be moved after heating. The microwave is available for heating, and the knife is present but not needed for this task since slicing is not required.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem heat_apple_and_place)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        apple - object
        sponge - object
        microwave - microwave
        table - object
        knife - knife
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 table)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isHot apple))
        (not (isSliced apple))
        (not (isCool apple))
        (not (isClean apple))
        (not (opened microwave))
        (openable microwave)
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
        ; The apple and sponge are on the table initially
        ; The domain doesn't have a predicate for "on table", so we assume the agent is at the table and can interact with objects there
        ; We'll use the fact that the agent is at the table to pick up the apple
    )
    (:goal (and
        (isHot apple)
        (atLocation agent1 table)
        ; The goal is to have the apple on the table, left of the sponge.
        ; Since the domain doesn't have spatial predicates, we assume that placing it back on the table after heating satisfies the spatial requirement
        ; as long as the agent places it in the correct position relative to the sponge (which is handled by the environment state after the action)
        ; We'll just ensure the apple is on the table and heated.
        ; Note: The domain does not support specifying "left of" spatial relationships, so we rely on the final placement being correct by the agent's action.
        ; For the purpose of this PDDL, we'll just ensure the apple is heated and placed on the table (which is the only receptacle-like surface we can use for placement).
        ; However, the domain does not define table as a receptacle, so we cannot use PutObjectInReceptacle on table.
        ; We must reinterpret: The task is to put the heated apple on the table. Since the table is not a receptacle in the domain, we cannot model placing it there directly.
        ; Alternative: We can only place objects in receptacles (microwave, fridge, sink). But the goal is to put it on the table.
        ; This is a limitation of the domain. We will assume that after heating, the agent places the apple back on the table (as a surface) and the domain's predicates do not prevent this.
        ; But the domain's actions require a receptacle for PutObjectInReceptacle. So we must adjust.
        ; Since the domain does not allow placing on non-receptacles, we cannot model the final placement on the table.
        ; This is a problem. We must either extend the domain or reinterpret.
        ; Given the constraints, we will assume that the table is not a receptacle and therefore we cannot place the apple there via the defined actions.
        ; This means the goal as stated cannot be achieved with the given domain.
        ; However, the instruction is clear: put the heated apple on the table.
        ; We must find a workaround. Perhaps the domain intends for the table to be a receptacle? But it is not defined as such.
        ; Alternatively, we can consider that the agent holds the apple after heating and then the goal is achieved when the apple is heated and the agent is at the table? But that doesn't place it on the table.
        ; Given the ambiguity, we will model the goal as the apple being heated and the agent being at the table, and assume that the placement on the table (left of sponge) is handled by the environment after the agent puts it down (even though the action is not defined).
        ; But this is not valid PDDL.
        ; Let's re-read the domain: The domain has only microwave, fridge, sink as receptacles. The table is not a receptacle.
        ; Therefore, we cannot place the apple on the table using PutObjectInReceptacle.
        ; We must conclude that the domain is insufficient for this task.
        ; However, for the sake of completing the task, we will assume that the table is a receptacle for this problem. We will add it to the receptacle type in the objects and init.
        ; But the domain file does not allow that. We are not allowed to change the domain.
        ; Alternative: We can use the fact that the agent can hold the apple and then the goal is to have the apple heated and the agent at the table? But the instruction says "put on the table".
        ; We are stuck.
        ; Let's look at the domain again: The domain has an action PutObjectInReceptacle that requires a receptacle. The table is not a receptacle.
        ; Therefore, we cannot model placing the apple on the table.
        ; This is a flaw in the domain for this task.
        ; Given the instructions, we must generate a PDDL problem. We will assume that the table is a receptacle for this problem, even though it is not defined in the domain, by adding it to the receptacle type in the objects and init.
        ; But the domain file does not allow us to add new types. We are constrained by the domain.
        ; We must find a way within the domain.
        ; Another idea: The goal might be interpreted as the apple being heated and then placed in a receptacle that is on the table? But there is no such receptacle.
        ; We decide to reinterpret the goal: The robot must heat the apple and then the apple is considered to be on the table (as per the initial state) after being heated, even though it was picked up and put back. But the domain doesn't have an action to put on non-receptacles.
        ; We will have to leave the placement part out and only require the apple to be heated and the agent to be at the table.
        ; But that does not satisfy the instruction.
        ; Given the constraints, we will generate the PDDL for heating the apple and then we will assume that the placement on the table is handled by the environment and not modeled in the PDDL.
        ; We will set the goal as the apple being heated and the agent being at the table.
        ; This is the best we can do.
        (isHot apple)
        (atLocation agent1 table)
    )
)
```

attempts.error:
Predicate argument has wrong type:
  atLocation sponge table: sponge is not a agent
  atLocation sink sink: sink is not a agent
  atLocation apple table: apple is not a agent
  atLocation microwave microwave: microwave is not a agent
  atLocation knife table: knife is not a agent

 )

        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a heated apple on the table, left of the sponge.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene depicts a kitchen environment with a dining table and a cooking area. The primary goal is to place a heated apple on the table to the left of a sponge. Below is a detailed breakdown of relevant objects, their types, states, and spatial relationships as defined by the domain.

---

**1. Objects and Their Types**

- **Receptacles** (objects that can hold other objects):
  - *Microwave*: A silver microwave mounted above the stove. It is a receptacle and is openable.
  - *Fridge*: Not visible in any of the images; assumed not present or not relevant in current view.
  - *Sink*: Part of the stove area; two metal sink basins are visible below the microwave. It is a receptacle and is openable.
  - *Table*: The green table surface is not explicitly defined as a receptacle in the domain, but objects are placed on it. For planning, it may serve as a destination for placing objects, though not a formal receptacle type in the PDDL.

- **Objects** (non-receptacle items):
  - *Apple*: A red apple resting on the table. It is an object and currently unsliced, unheated, and uncooled.
  - *Knife*: A silver knife with a black handle, lying on the table near the apple. It is an object and also a knife type as per domain.
  - *Sponge*: A green rectangular sponge with a yellow border, located on the table. It is an object.
  - *Wine Bottle*: A dark green bottle, standing upright on the table. Not relevant to the task.
  - *Cabbage/Lettuce*: A large green vegetable on the table. Not relevant.
  - *Fork*: A silver fork next to the apple. Not relevant.
  - *Pan*: A brown grill pan on the right side of the table. Not relevant.
  - *Pencil*: A red pencil next to the sponge. Not relevant.
  - *Spatula*: A spatula with a black handle, lying on the sponge. Not relevant.
  - *Bread*: A loaf of bread on the table in the second image. Not relevant.
  - *Cup*: A white cup visible under the table in the second image. Not relevant.

---

**2. Quantities of Each Object Type**

- Receptacles: 2 (Microwave, Sink)
- Knives: 1 (silver knife with black handle)
- Objects (relevant to task): 3 (Apple, Sponge, Knife)
- Other objects: Multiple (wine bottle, cabbage, fork, pan, pencil, spatula, bread, cup) — not relevant to current goal.

---

**3. Spatial Relationships Between Objects**

- The *apple* is on the table, to the right of the *sponge*.
- The *sponge* is on the table, with a *spatula* lying on top of it and a *red pencil* next to it.
- The *knife* is on the table, near the apple and to the left of the sponge.
- The *microwave* is above the stove, which has two sink basins below it.
- The *sink* is directly below the microwave and to the left of the stove burners.
- The *table* is in the foreground with chairs behind it; the stove and microwave are in the background.

---

**4. Current State of Each Object Relevant to the Planning Task**

- *Apple*: Not held by any agent, not heated, not cooled, not sliced. It is on the table.
- *Sponge*: Not held by any agent. It is on the table and serves as a reference point for placement.
- *Knife*: Not held by any agent. It is on the table and can be used to slice the apple if needed (though slicing is not required for the task).
- *Microwave*: Not opened, not toggled, not currently holding any object. It is a receptacle and can be opened.
- *Sink*: Not opened, not holding any object. It is a receptacle and can be opened.

---

**5. Colors, Positions, and Other Visual Properties**

- *Apple*: Bright red, round, located on the right side of the table, near the center.
- *Sponge*: Green with yellow border, rectangular, located on the table to the left of the apple.
- *Knife*: Silver blade, black handle, lying horizontally on the table, near the apple.
- *Microwave*: Silver with black control panel, mounted above the stove.
- *Sink*: Two metal basins, shiny, located below the microwave.
- *Table*: Green surface with a checkered pattern on part of it, flat surface holding multiple objects.
- *Chairs*: Two wooden chairs behind the table, not relevant to task.

---

**Summary for Planning**

The robot must first go to the apple, pick it up, then go to the microwave, open it, put the apple inside, heat it, remove it, and finally place it on the table to the left of the sponge. The knife and sponge are static reference points; the sponge defines the target location. The microwave must be opened and toggled to heat the apple. No slicing or cleaning is required. The sink is not needed for this task.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem heat_apple_and_place)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        apple - object
        sponge - object
        knife - knife
        microwave - microwave
        sink - sink
        table - object  ; Note: table is not a receptacle per domain, but we'll use it for location reference
    )
    (:init
        ; Initial locations: objects are on the table
        (atLocation robot table)
        (atLocation apple table)
        (atLocation sponge table)
        (atLocation knife table)
        (atLocation microwave microwave)  ; microwave is at its own location
        (atLocation sink sink)            ; sink is at its own location

        ; Microwave is openable but initially closed
        (openable microwave)
        (not (opened microwave))

        ; Sink is openable but initially closed
        (openable sink)
        (not (opened sink))

        ; Apple is not heated, not cooled, not sliced
        (not (isHot apple))
        (not (isCool apple))
        (not (isSliced apple))

        ; Robot is not holding anything
        (not (holdsAny robot))

        ; Apple is to the right of the sponge (spatial relationship for goal)
        ; This is a conceptual constraint; we'll handle placement via actions
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (isHot apple)
            (atLocation apple table)
            ; The goal is to place the apple on the table to the left of the sponge.
            ; Since the domain doesn't have spatial predicates, we interpret this as:
            ; After heating, the apple must be placed on the table, and we assume the robot
            ; can position it appropriately relative to the sponge.
            ; We'll rely on the final placement action to satisfy this.
            (not (holdsAny robot))
        )
    )
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem heat_apple_and_place)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        apple - object
        sponge - object
        knife - knife
        microwave - microwave
        sink - sink
        table - object  ; Note: table is not a receptacle per domain, but we'll use it for location reference
    )
    (:init
        ; Initial locations: objects are on the table
        (atLocation robot table)
        (atLocation apple table)
        (atLocation sponge table)
        (atLocation knife table)
        (atLocation microwave microwave)  ; microwave is at its own location
        (atLocation sink sink)            ; sink is at its own location

        ; Microwave is openable but initially closed
        (openable microwave)
        (not (opened microwave))

        ; Sink is openable but initially closed
        (openable sink)
        (not (opened sink))

        ; Apple is not heated, not cooled, not sliced
        (not (isHot apple))
        (not (isCool apple))
        (not (isSliced apple))

        ; Robot is not holding anything
        (not (holdsAny robot))

        ; Apple is to the right of the sponge (spatial relationship for goal)
        ; This is a conceptual constraint; we'll handle placement via actions
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (isHot apple)
            (atLocation apple table)
            ; The goal is to place the apple on the table to the left of the sponge.
            ; Since the domain doesn't have spatial predicates, we interpret this as:
            ; After heating, the apple must be placed on the table, and we assume the robot
            ; can position it appropriately relative to the sponge.
            ; We'll rely on the final placement action to satisfy this.
            (not (holdsAny robot))
        )
    )
)

attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)

```pddl
        (inReceptacle knife table)
        (inReceptacle spatula book)
        (inReceptacle fork table)
        (inReceptacle pencil table)
        (inReceptacle lettuce table)
        (inReceptacle potato table)
        (inReceptacle tomato table)
        (inReceptacle spoon table)
        (inReceptacle bottle table)
        (inReceptacle book table)
        (inReceptacle pan table)
        (inReceptacle plate table)
        (not (isHot apple))
        (not (isSliced apple))
        (not (isClean apple))
        (not (isCool apple))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle apple table)
            (isHot apple)
            (atLocation robot table)
            (forall (?o - object)
                (when (and (inReceptacle ?o table) (not (= ?o apple)))
                    (not (atLocation robot ?o))))
        )
    )
)
```
final.problem:
(define (problem heat_apple_and_place)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        microwave - microwave
        sink - sink
        fridge - fridge
        table - receptacle
        pan - receptacle
        plate - receptacle
        cup - receptacle
        drawer - receptacle
        apple - object
        knife - knife
        spatula - object
        fork - object
        pencil - object
        lettuce - object
        potato - object
        tomato - object
        spoon - object
        bottle - object
        book - object
        stove - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable microwave)
        (not (opened microwave))
        (atLocation robot table)
        (inReceptacle apple table)
        (inReceptacle knife table)
        (inReceptacle spatula book)
        (inReceptacle fork table)
        (inReceptacle pencil table)
        (inReceptacle lettuce table)
        (inReceptacle potato table)
        (inReceptacle tomato table)
        (inReceptacle spoon table)
        (inReceptacle bottle table)
        (inReceptacle book table)
        (inReceptacle pan table)
        (inReceptacle plate table)
        (not (isHot apple))
        (not (isSliced apple))
        (not (isClean apple))
        (not (isCool apple))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle apple table)
            (isHot apple)
            (atLocation robot table)
            (forall (?o - object)
                (when (and (inReceptacle ?o table) (not (= ?o apple)))
                    (not (atLocation robot ?o))))
        )
    )
)
